meta.name: CD-RISC-2 Connor-Davidson Resilience Scale
meta.description: The CD-RISC-2 is a 2-item ultra-brief version of the Connor-Davidson Resilience Scale, extracting the two most core items from the original 25-item scale for rapid assessment of resilience to stress and adversity.
meta.author: Vaishnavi, Connor & Davidson (2007)
meta.reference: Vaishnavi S, Connor K, Davidson JRT. An abbreviated version of the Connor-Davidson Resilience Scale (CD-RISC), the CD-RISC2: psychometric properties and applications in psychopharmacological trials. Psychiatry Res. 2007;152(2-3):293-297.
meta.instruction: Please indicate how much the following statements have applied to you over the past month.

questions.text: Tend to bounce back after illness or hardship

ranges.label: Low Resilience
ranges.description: Your resilience level is low. You may find it quite difficult to cope with change and stress. This indicates that your current sense of well-being and life satisfaction is relatively low. Focusing on self-care and lifestyle adjustments may be beneficial.
suggestions: Seek social support; do not face difficulties alone
Learn techniques and strategies for adapting to change
Consider professional help to strengthen resilience
Start with small challenges and gradually build coping ability
Try doing one fulfilling activity each day

ranges.label: Moderate Resilience
ranges.description: Your resilience is at a moderate level. You can cope with stress and change reasonably well in some situations. This level reflects a generally positive psychological state, where you can experience meaning and satisfaction in most aspects of life.
suggestions: Strengthen resilience through regular exercise and healthy habits
Practice positive self-talk and cognitive reframing

ranges.label: High Resilience
ranges.description: You have high resilience and can effectively adapt to change and recover from stress. Your well-being and positive experiences are at a high level, reflecting strong psychological health and life adaptation.
suggestions: Your resilience qualities can help and encourage those around you
While helping others, also attend to your own self-care
Keep up the habits and activities that bring you happiness
Share your positive experiences with others
